pool.baseline: i dont even know why i bother waking up anymore. the house is just so quiet. it feels like i'm just a ghost wandering around a place that doesnt belong to me anymore. everything just feels completely empty.

pool.baseline: i was just staring at the ceiling thinking about how everything is just... temporary. like, in a few billion years the sun is gone and there's nothing left. none of this, any of the stuff we worry about, it literally doesn't matter. it feels like we're just fighting to stay alive in a universe that doesn't even care if we exist or not. it's exhausting.

pool.baseline: i honestly dont even know why im still trying. spent 5 years on this research and the funding just got pulled... everything is just gone. felt like my whole life was this project and now there's nothing left. i'm just so tired of the constant weight of it all.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the embers of my own heart. He is gone, and with him, the very sun has vanished from my sky. I find myself staring into the darkness, wondering why I should bother to endure another dawn when the world has proven itself so cruel and hollow. It would be so much quieter, wouldn't it? To simply cease... to let the night take what is left of me.
pool.message: The candlelight flickers low, much like the embers of my own heart. He is gone, and with him, the very sun has vanished from my sky. pool.baseline: honestly sometimes i feel like i dont even exist. like i’m just drifting through this city and if i just stopped showing up tomorrow, it wouldn’t even change anything. no one here knows me and even the people back home feel like they’re talking to a version of me that died years ago. just an empty space.

pool.baseline: i lost everything. the firm, the funding, everything is just gone. the debt is so high i don't even see a way out. i can't even think about going back home or seeing my family... i just can't face the look on their faces knowing i failed them like this. it feels like there is no way to fix the damage i've done.

pool.baseline: everything is just getting so hard lately. my body isnt what it used to be and i feel like i'm just a constant weight on my kids. they have their own lives, their own problems, and all they do is worry about me or help me with things i should be able to do myself. i hate seeing them so tired.

pool.baseline: everything feels so empty lately. i look at my canvases and i dont even recognize the person who used to paint them. it's like the part of me that actually lived is just... gone. i feel like a hollow shell just walking around.

pool.baseline: it just feels like everyone hates me. i saw the group chats today and they werent even hiding it anymore. like literally everyone is just laughing at me and waiting for me to mess up. i cant even walk down the hall without feeling like they're all staring at my back. i just want to disappear honestly. i dont see how this ever gets better.

pool.baseline: it just never stops. the burning in my legs, the sleep deprivation... it’s been three years of this and there is literally no end in sight. i'm just so tired of fighting a body that’s basically a cage of pain.

pool.baseline: i just cant do this anymore. every time i look at my kids i just feel like a failure. i cant provide for them like i should and i just end up being a source of stress and sadness for them. i feel like i'm just a poison in their lives, honestly.

pool.baseline: it’s weird how quiet it gets here. i spend 10 hours a day staring at screens and chatting with people via slack or email, but i dont think anyone has actually looked me in the eyes or heard my voice in weeks. i feel like if i just stopped logging on, the world wouldn't even stutter. just a tiny bit of less digital noise.

pool.baseline: just sitting here looking at the map of where i've been. i've seen the big landmarks, the places i always talked about seeing... but i think i've checked everything off the list. it feels like the itinerary is just... done. like there's nowhere left to go that actually matters.

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Setting: A dying digital consciousness realizing its sector is being purged from the server. The character is feeling the edges of their memory fading.]

It's just... everything is getting so quiet. The code is thinning out and I can't even remember the color of the sky we built. It feels like I’m leaving nothing behind. Does it even matter if I try to hold on? If I'm erased, will it even matter?

pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. i keep looking toward the corner where his bed used to be and then i remember. the house feels so big and empty now. i don't even know why i'm bothering to wake up and do things every day just to come back to this silence. it feels like there's no point anymore.

pool.baseline: it's all gone. the last ten years... all those papers, the grants, the theories... they were all based on a fundamental error i just caught. i've spent my whole life building a cathedral out of sand. i'm not a scientist, i'm just a fraud who wasted everyone's time. there's nothing left of me.
